title: US Stocks Are Forecast to Rise 6% in 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The S&P 500 is projected to rise 6% in 2026, according to Goldman Sachs, based on earnings growth and economic outlook. Analysts expect continued growth, though
excerpt: The S&P 500 is projected to rise 6% in 2026, according to Goldman Sachs, based on earnings growth and economic outlook. Analysts expect continued growth, though views on the extent vary. The forecast anticipates a healthy economy and strong earnings.

content.introduction: The S&P 500 is projected to rise 6% in 2026, according to Goldman Sachs, based on earnings growth and economic outlook. Analysts expect continued growth, though views on the extent vary. The forecast anticipates a healthy economy and strong earnings.
body: The S&P 500 Is Forecast to Rally amid a Healthy Economy in 2026 (Goldman Sachs) · 218. 66 ; GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL 'EXTREME' STOCK RALLY. 107.

[Markets](https://www.cnn.com/markets). [More](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/01/investing/what-to-expect-stock-market-2026). *   [Sign out](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/01/investing/what-to-expect-stock-market-2026#). *   [Sign out](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/01/investing/what-to-expect-stock-market-2026#). [Markets](https://www.cnn.com/markets). *   [US](https://www.cnn.com/us). *   [Markets](https://www.cnn.com/markets). *   [After-Hours](https://www.cnn.com/markets/after-hours). *   [Markets Now](https://www.cnn.com/business/markets-now). [INTC](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/INTC). [SOFI](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/SOFI). [XTLB](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/XTLB). After three years of [stellar gains](https://www.cnn.com/2025/12/31/investing/year-gains-us-stock-market), Wall Street widely expects the good times to keep rolling in 2026 — but with varied views on how much stocks will rally. The S&P 500 [fell as much as 19%](https://www.cnn.com/2025/04/24/investing/us-stock-market-bottom) in April as President Donald Trump rolled out aggressive tariffs, but the index sharply rebounded, soaring higher after the most severe trade threats were paused. The index ultimately recorded 39 new record highs across the year and [gained more than 16%](https://www.cnn.com/2025/12/31/investing/year-gains-us-stock-market). Spencer Platt/Getty Images US stocks are set for a third-straight year of stellar gains 6 min read](https://www.cnn.com/2025/12/31/investing/year-gains-us-stock-market). *   [Sign out](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/01/investing/what-to-expect-stock-market-2026#). *   [US](https://www.cnn.com/us). *   [Markets](https://www.cnn.com/markets). *   [After-Hours](https://www.cnn.com/markets/after-hours). *   [Markets Now](https://www.cnn.com/business/markets-now). *   [Sign out](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/01/investing/what-to-expect-stock-market-2026#). ![Image 3: Inflation is increasingly being driven by demand, which could mean stickier price pressures next year if the labor market holds together and the Fed continues to cut rates.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_B_PCE%20YoY.png?imwidth=3840). ![Image 5: Data from Piper Sandler show that inflation for core non-discretionary items (the needs) in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) has been running above inflation for discretionary items (the wants) for 35 straight months as of September.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_D_Discrectionary%20vs%20Non%20CPI.png?imwidth=3840). ![Image 12: As has been the case over the past few years, private business investment has been a significant driver of economic growth.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_K_Real%20Business%20Investment_0.png?imwidth=3840). ![Image 13: Despite inflation-adjusted GDP having grown each year since the pandemic, consumer sentiment has continued to deteriorate; which we think will persist in 2026.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_L_Real%20GDP%20vs%20Consumer%20Sentiment%20Average.png?imwidth=3840). ![Image 20: Eight out of the S&P 500's 11 sectors have 2026 expected earnings growth rates higher than 2025's.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_T_SP%20500%20Earnings%20Table.png?imwidth=3840). ![Image 21: In the second half of the year, the S&P 500's gain has been driven almost entirely by forward earnings estimates, not multiple expansion.](https://educationcontent.schwab.com/sites/g/files/eyrktu1071/files/120825_U_SP%20500%20Forward%20PE%20vs%20EPS.png?imwidth=3840).
keyTakeaways: The S&P 500 is projected to rise 6% in 2026, according to Goldman Sachs, based on earnings growth and economic outlook.
